resources.string: The app runs in the background and analyzes incoming SMS for signs of fraud.
⚠️ Be careful: never share your verification code over the phone. If you suspect anything, hang up and call the BANK, Government Agencies, Police at their official number, or your Relatives.
«Display over other apps» permission is not granted — the red warning may not appear over the call screen.
Tap — the system will request several permissions one by one. Choose «Allow» for each. At the end, the «Display over other apps» screen will open — enable the toggle there.
Last step: display over other apps
Needed so the red warning appears over the call screen.
Delete all detection records from this device.
All detection records will be permanently deleted. Continue?
Nothing yet. No suspicious events detected.
Do NOT share the code by phone!
Hang up and call the bank at the official number on the back of your card.
Neither the bank, the police, nor government services EVER ask for codes by phone.
Before we start — a short agreement.
✅ Everything runs locally on your device.
📵 The content of your SMS is not sent anywhere.
📊 Only one anonymous signal is sent to the server — confirmation of accepting this agreement.
⚠️ This is an auxiliary tool, not a replacement for your vigilance.
I have read the Privacy Policy and Terms, and I accept them.

ScamGuard recognizes a common phone scam pattern: a call combined with a confirmation code arriving by SMS. When it detects this combination, the app shows a warning — don\'t share the code with anyone, and call back only using the official number. All messages are processed only on your device, nothing is sent anywhere.
What should I do if I suspect a scammer?

Hang up immediately — don\'t continue the conversation and don\'t share any codes, passwords, or card details. Then call the organization back yourself, using the official number — not the one the caller gave you, but one from the organization\'s website, the back of your card, or the "Call" button in a ScamGuard warning. Never call back a number left by a suspicious caller or received in an SMS.
What is the "Call" button in the warning for, and can I trust that number?

When the app recognizes an organization (bank, operator, etc.), the warning shows a button with its official number — for example, 1313 for MAIB. These numbers were manually verified against official organization websites, not taken from the suspicious SMS itself. Tapping it opens the dial screen — the call isn\'t placed automatically, you tap "Call" yourself once you\'ve confirmed everything is correct.
Why am I not getting any warnings at all?

The most common reason is that not all required permissions have been granted. The home screen will show a yellow card, "Set up ScamGuard" or "Last step: display over other apps", as long as at least one permission is missing. Tap it and grant everything the system asks for — especially "Display over other apps": without it, the yellow ring-time banner won\'t appear at all. If that card isn\'t showing and the status is green, protection is set up correctly and the issue is something else (see the next question).
Is it true that SMS content is never transmitted anywhere?

Yes. All SMS analysis happens locally on your device. The app has no technical way to send message content anywhere — the ScamGuard server does not receive or store SMS. The only network request is an anonymous confirmation of accepting the terms of use on first launch.
Why didn\'t a warning appear sometimes?

ScamGuard is an auxiliary tool, not a guarantee of protection. A warning appears for a specific combination of signs (code in SMS + call). New scam schemes, unusual code wording, or SMS delivery delays from the carrier may mean the app stays silent. Stay alert regardless — especially if the caller is rushing you and asking for a code.
Why does the app need so many permissions?

• SMS reception — to analyze incoming messages for signs of fraud (on device, not sent anywhere).
• Phone state — to know if a call is currently active.
• Call management — allows the "Hang up" button in the notification to actually end the call.
• Display over other apps — to show the red warning over the call screen.

Everything runs locally. SMS content is not sent anywhere.
I found a bug or a translation error — where do I report it?

Go to Settings → «Contact» section → «Report a bug» — this opens an email with the app version and device info already filled in, just describe the issue in your own words. If you noticed a translation mistake (in Russian or Romanian) — mention it in the email, this is especially valuable since the app is translated manually. There may also be a Telegram community link there, once it\'s set up — you can message directly there instead.
Do not share the code with anyone!
